motivation: The user needed to install/compile/run the project and persistently configure it to connect to a third-party Anthropic-compatible API using a provided key and base URL.
approach: Configured the project to use a user-level Claude CLI settings file at /Users/jqwang/.claude/settings.json, setting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and using a root ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL without the /v1 suffix so the CLI’s built-in path concatenation produces the correct /v1/messages endpoint.
challenges: Initial failures were caused by providing a base_url that already included /v1, resulting in double-prefix requests (/v1/v1/messages); an alternate endpoint (lingkong.xyz/v1) returned 502 from the server.
tradeoffs: Chose user-level config (outside git) for quick, persistent setup, but it isn’t tracked in the repo and may require replication on other machines/environments.
outcome: A working user-level configuration was put in place (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL set to https://code.pumpkinai.vip), and the CLI was validated via a test prompt returning OK; the key insight is that ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL must be the root host (no /v1) to avoid double pathing.
